mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in nonfarm payrolls above 135,000 indicates strong labor market demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS > 135000 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 20d return volatility exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production above 114.5 signals robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO > 114.5 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 5d return correlation with INDPRO < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in CPI inflation and falling Fed funds rates creates stagflationary pressure, prompting equity market risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in CPIAUCSL > 208.0 and FEDFUNDS < 5.02 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or 60d return volatility < 1.8%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tight labor markets driving wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE < 4.5 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or 20d return correlation with UNRATE > -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1,380 thousand indicates strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST > 1380 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 20d return volatility > 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDP and INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or 60d return correlation < 0.30.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation above 208.0 signals central bank policy inaction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to real rate compression.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in CPIAUCSL > 208.0 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or 5d return volatility < 1.5%.
